prevalence.trend: increasing in urban office workers and with diabetes prevalence

vulnerablePopulations: office workers with high keyboard use
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical diagnosis supported by nerve conduction studies when available; exclude radiculopathy

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Variable; dependent on access to splints, injections, and surgery

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: NCS access concentrated in urban centers; many diagnoses are clinical
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Splinting, activity modification, NSAIDs; steroid injection; surgical release for severe cases
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Out-of-pocket costs and delayed access to surgery in many settings
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Availability of low-cost conservative management and expanding orthopedic services
